source_note: Preliminary Order of the Public Utility Commission of Texas identifying the issues (including prudence of fuel and purchased-power costs) in El Paso Electric Company's application to reconcile Texas-jurisdictional fuel, fuel-related, and purchased-power expenses for the reconciliation period April 1, 2022 through March 31, 2024. PUC Docket No. 57149 / SOAH Docket No. 473-25-05084, filed November 14, 2024. Listed for reference (metadata-only). Source: PUCT Interchange, document interchange.puc.texas.gov/Documents/57149_34_1442512.PDF.

Direct Testimony and Attachments of Evan D. Evans on behalf of the Office of Public Utility Counsel (OPUC, the statutory residential and small-commercial consumer advocate), challenging cost recovery in El Paso Electric Company's fuel-cost reconciliation, PUC Docket No. 57149 / SOAH Docket No. 473-25-05084, filed March 25, 2025. Listed for reference (metadata-only). Source: PUCT Interchange, document interchange.puc.texas.gov/Documents/57149_113_1483132.PDF.

Redacted Direct Testimony and Workpapers of Scott Norwood on behalf of the City of El Paso, addressing re-allocation of purchased-power-agreement energy costs, imputed capacity charge credits, and Newman Unit 6 fuel costs in El Paso Electric Company's fuel-cost reconciliation, PUC Docket No. 57149 / SOAH Docket No. 473-25-05084, filed March 25, 2025. Listed for reference (metadata-only). Source: PUCT Interchange, document interchange.puc.texas.gov/Documents/57149_114_1483150.PDF.

Direct Testimony of Vonetta Jackson, Rate Regulation Division, Public Utility Commission of Texas (Commission Staff), in El Paso Electric Company's fuel-cost reconciliation, PUC Docket No. 57149 / SOAH Docket No. 473-25-05084, filed April 1, 2025. Listed for reference (metadata-only). Source: PUCT Interchange, document interchange.puc.texas.gov/Documents/57149_118_1485108.PDF.

Commission Order reconciling Southwestern Public Service Company's (SPS) fuel and purchased-power costs for 2002-2003, PUCT Docket 29801 (SOAH 473-04-6558), Item 432, issued December 19, 2005; 21 pp. A concluded contested fuel-cost reconciliation (complements the still-open El Paso Electric 57149). PDF: interchange.puc.texas.gov/Documents/29801_432_499091.PDF. Listed for reference (metadata-only).
